certificate_pinning_bypass.description: Post-pinning-bypass payloads for testing mobile API backends once traffic is intercepted via Burp proxy (Frida/Objection already applied)

payloads.payload:
GET /api/v1/config HTTP/1.1
Host: api.target.com
X-Api-Key: HARDCODED_KEY
payloads.description: Replay hardcoded API key from decompiled binary — test if server validates key origin
Replay captured JWT after logout — test token revocation enforcement
Debug endpoint probe — mobile backends often leave /debug, /status, /health exposed

payloads.payload:
GET /api/internal/admin HTTP/1.1
Host: api.target.com
X-Mobile-App: true
payloads.description: Internal API probe with mobile app header — test for hidden admin endpoints
Old app version header — test if old API versions with less security are still accepted
Android App Links asset file — reveals associated app package names and certificate fingerprints

api_key_extraction.description: Payloads to test hardcoded API keys, secrets, and credentials found in intercepted mobile traffic or decompiled binaries

payloads.description: Base64-decoded basic auth from binary — test:test default credential probe
Google Maps API key format probe — check if server-side restriction exists
Parse Server keys from decompiled app — test direct backend access bypassing mobile client
Firebase Cloud Messaging server key — test if FCM key from app can send push to arbitrary devices
AWS keys from mobile traffic — probe for direct S3/DynamoDB access bypassing backend
GraphQL introspection with extracted API key — enumerate full schema accessible to mobile client
API key listing endpoint — test if mobile token grants access to key management

broken_auth.description: Mobile API broken authentication payloads — JWT manipulation, weak tokens, session fixation targeting mobile backends

payloads.description: JWT alg:none bypass — remove signature, set algorithm to none for admin role
JWT role tampering — change role claim to admin, resign with common secrets
Zero device ID — test if device binding is enforced on JWT validation
OTP brute force — test rate limiting on mobile OTP verification (common 4-6 digit codes)
Session fixation via device_id — bind victim session to attacker device
Refresh token replay — test if refresh tokens are single-use and properly rotated
Token-user mismatch — send valid token with different user_id to test binding
JWT algorithm confusion RS256->HS256 — sign with public key as HMAC secret

insecure_data_storage.description: Payloads to detect excessive data exposure, PII leakage, and insecure data handling in mobile API responses

payloads.description: Full profile response — check for excessive fields (SSN, DOB, payment info, internal IDs) beyond what the mobile UI displays
User listing endpoint — test if mobile user token grants access to other users' PII
IDOR on user profile — access another user's data with own token (OWASP M1)
Verbose include parameter — test if API returns full transaction details including card numbers

payloads.payload:
GET /api/v1/user/profile HTTP/1.1
Authorization: Bearer VALID_TOKEN
Accept: application/xml
payloads.description: XML content negotiation — test if XML response includes additional fields not in JSON response
Client log endpoint — test if server stores and exposes mobile client logs containing sensitive data
Data export endpoint — test for mass PII export capability via mobile API

api_versioning_abuse.description: API versioning abuse payloads — access deprecated or beta endpoints with weaker security controls

payloads.description: v1 endpoint — older version may lack rate limiting, auth checks, or input validation
v0 pre-release endpoint — often has debug features and no auth
Beta API path — test for unreleased features with incomplete access controls
Version header downgrade — send v1 version header to v2 endpoint for parser confusion

payloads.payload:
GET /api/v1/user/profile HTTP/1.1
Host: api.target.com
Accept: application/vnd.api.v1+json
payloads.description: Content negotiation versioning — request old API version via Accept header
Future version probe — test if v3/v4/v5 exists with different access controls
Staging API host — mobile apps sometimes hardcode staging URLs with relaxed auth
Date-based API versioning — request old snapshot with deprecated security model

deeplink_abuse.description: Deep link and intent scheme injection payloads for mobile app URI handlers intercepted in traffic

payloads.description: OAuth callback deep link hijack — inject redirect parameter to steal auth token
Android Intent URI — force target app to open attacker URL via intent scheme
WebView URL injection — open attacker page in app's trusted WebView context
JavaScript URI in WebView deep link — XSS in mobile WebView if javascript: scheme not filtered
Payment deep link tampering — modify amount/recipient in payment flow redirect
OAuth callback without state validation — test if mobile OAuth flow is vulnerable to CSRF
Password reset deep link — test if reset token in deep link can be replayed or lacks binding

push_notification_abuse.description: Push notification token hijack and abuse payloads for FCM/APNs endpoints found in mobile API traffic

payloads.description: FCM token rebinding — register victim's push token to attacker account to intercept notifications

payloads.payload:
POST /api/v1/notifications/send HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Bearer USER_TOKEN

{"to":"VICTIM_FCM_TOKEN","title":"Test","body":"Phish"}
payloads.description: Direct push send — test if mobile user token allows sending push to arbitrary FCM tokens
Device token IDOR — register attacker's push token for victim user to receive their notifications
Notification history — test for sensitive data in push notification logs (OTP codes, messages)
Multiple device registration — test if server limits device count per account (device farm abuse)
Device deregistration IDOR — unregister victim's device to deny push notifications
